---
title: "Foreword"
subtitle: "Comparative Analysis Framework"
---

This book presents a systematic framework for evaluating social media platforms across multiple regions, with a focus on accountability, transparency, and user protection.

This evaluation system assesses platforms across two critical dimensions:

- **User-Generated Content (UGC)**: Content moderation policies, reporting mechanisms, and transparency

- **Advertising (ADS)**: Ad transparency tools, political ad labeling, and targeting restrictions


## How to Cite

If you use this report in your research or work, please cite as:

```bibtex
@techreport{santini2026datanotfound,
  title        = {Data Not Found: Social Media Data Transparency for Information Integrity},
  author       = {Santini, Rose Marie and Leal, Humberto and Salles, Diogo and Bel{\'i}sario, Adriano and Mattos, Bruno and Pinho, Diego},
  year         = {2026},
  institution  = {NetLab UFRJ \& Minderoo Centre for Technology and Democracy},
}
```

**APA Style:**
Santini, R. M., Leal, H., Salles, D., Belisário, A., Mattos, B., & Pinho, D. (2026). *Data Not Found: Social Media Data Transparency for Information Integrity*. NetLab UFRJ & Minderoo Centre for Technology and Democracy.

**ABNT Style:**
SANTINI, Rose Marie et al. *Data Not Found: Social Media Data Transparency for Information Integrity*. NetLab UFRJ & Minderoo Centre for Technology and Democracy, 2026.
